# Work Packet ALB-015 — Manifest → Compile Pipeline

## 1. TASK

Turn `charter/ALBION.md` from a hand-maintained file into a compiled artifact: decompose it into ordered section fragments, register everything in `manifest/albion-manifest.yaml`, and ship a compiler with a drift gate.

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME

- `manifest/albion-manifest.yaml` — single source-of-truth registry.
- `manifest/sections/<nn>-<id>.md` — charter fragments (one per `albion:section` marker, plus the preamble).
- `bin/albion-compile` — reassembles the charter; `--check` mode exits non-zero on drift.
- `tests/test_manifest.sh` — structural + drift checks.
- One sanctioned edit inside `charter/ALBION.md` (see MUST DO).

## 3. CONTEXT

- **Read first:** `charter/ALBION.md` (note the `<!-- albion:section X -->` HTML-comment markers), `CONVENTIONS.md` (portability is enforced), `docs/proposal/albion-proposal.md` §1 (manifest role) and §3 (compile-time sizing), `bin/albion-doctor` (style reference for bin/ tools).
- **Decomposition rule:** split the charter at each `albion:section` marker line. Each fragment file *contains* its marker line and everything up to (not including) the next marker. Content before the first marker is fragment `00-preamble.md`. Concatenating all fragments in manifest order MUST reproduce the charter byte-for-byte (after the sanctioned edit below).
- **Manifest format:** the file is named `.yaml` but MUST be a strict line-based subset parseable without PyYAML or yq (neither is a dependency; python3 stdlib only). Document the subset in a header comment. It must express: a schema/version line; a `charter` list of `{id, file}` entries in compile order with `target: claude-code`; a `skills` list and an `agents` list naming each shipped plugin asset with its path. Keep it flat and boring — no anchors, no nesting beyond one level of list items with `key: value` pairs.
- **Compiler contract:** `bin/albion-compile` (bash + python3 stdlib, same conventions as the other bin/ tools): default mode writes `charter/ALBION.md` from the fragments; `--check` recompiles to a temp file and diffs against the committed charter, exit 0 clean / non-zero with a short factual report on drift. Resolve all paths relative to the repo root derived from the script's own location (symlink-safe, like `bin/albion`).

## 4. MUST DO

- The sanctioned charter edit: in the preamble HTML comment, replace the sentence stating the file is hand-maintained with one stating it is compiled from `manifest/` and must be edited there (`bin/albion-compile` regenerates it). No other charter change of any kind — the compiled output must be byte-identical to the committed file.
- `tests/test_manifest.sh`, wired into `bash tests/run.sh`, asserting: manifest parses under the documented subset; every `charter` entry's fragment file exists and every fragment file in `manifest/sections/` is registered (bidirectional); `bin/albion-compile --check` exits 0 on the committed tree; mutating a temp *copy* of a fragment and compiling to a temp target produces a diff (the check is not hollow — do this against temp paths, never the real tree); every path in `skills`/`agents` lists exists on disk, and every `plugin/skills/*/SKILL.md` and `plugin/agents/*.md` on disk is listed (bidirectional).
- Follow `CONVENTIONS.md`: identical behavior on macOS (BSD) and ubuntu (GNU); `python3` for anything sed/stat-fragile; no new dependencies.
- Run the full suite yourself before finishing.

## 5. MUST NOT DO

- Do NOT change charter prose, structure, or markers beyond the single sanctioned comment edit.
- Do NOT touch `plugin/` contents (read-only for listing), `bin/albion`, `bin/albion-doctor`, hooks, or state.
- Do NOT add a doctor check (that is ALB-016's lane) or a marketplace/plugin.json.
- Do NOT introduce PyYAML, yq, or any dependency.
- Do NOT modify anything outside the paths in EXPECTED OUTCOME.
- Do NOT run git state-changing commands.

## 6. TOOLS ALLOWED

File creation/editing and shell execution within the repository workspace only.

## 7. SUCCESS CRITERIA

- [ ] `bash tests/run.sh` shows `test_manifest.sh` passing; no other test regresses.
- [ ] `bin/albion-compile --check` exits 0; `cat manifest/sections/*.md` in manifest order equals `charter/ALBION.md` byte-for-byte.
- [ ] Removing a fragment from a temp manifest copy and compiling to a temp target demonstrably shrinks the output (compile-time sizing works).
- [ ] CI-equivalent shellcheck clean; `git status` shows only the allowed paths.
